findings.title: Untested upstream-bootstrap boundary failure branch
findings.details: assembleControlPlanePermissionAuditAccessContext throws 'requires completed CP00-068 bootstrap boundary' when permission_matrix_rows_bound / view_decision_binding_bound / search_decision_binding_bound / mutation_decision_binding_bound / no_raw_permission_policy / no_internal_audit_payload are not true or ldip_implementation_allowed is not false, but no negative test flips any of these. Drift tests cover only pack_id and next_subphase.
findings.recommendation: Add a case that sets one bootstrap binding flag false on the upstream input and asserts the 'completed CP00-068 bootstrap boundary' throw.

findings.title: Untested non-object boundaryClaims guard
findings.details: The guard rejecting array/string/non-object boundaryClaims ('boundary claims must be an object when provided') is not exercised by a test.
findings.recommendation: Add a throws-case passing an array or string as boundaryClaims; low priority.

findings.title: forbiddenBoundarySuiteClaims reused-by-reference, not asserted equal to contract list
findings.details: policy.forbiddenBoundarySuiteClaims aliases CONTROL_PLANE_PERMISSION_AUDIT_INTEGRATION_BOOTSTRAP_POLICY.forbiddenBoundarySuiteClaims rather than the contract's explicit forbidden_boundary_suite_claims; the policy validator only checks it is non-empty, not that it matches the contract enumeration. Risk is non-exploitable because the assembler rejects ANY truthy claim (forbidden -> 'cannot claim', otherwise -> 'cannot accept unknown claim'), so an incomplete forbidden list cannot weaken the fail-closed boundary; it only changes which error message fires.
findings.recommendation: Optionally assert the policy/contract forbidden lists are equal to keep documented and enforced lists from drifting; not required for closeout.

findings.title: requested_pack_id lags one pack (CP00-068)
findings.details: requested_pack_id=CP00-068 while planned_pack_id/pack_id=CP00-069 with deviation_from_plan=false and correction_reason 'shifted to CP00-069 by live sequential cursor'. This matches the established live-cursor pattern from prior packs (CP00-068 requested CP00-067) and is accepted by the validator; informational only.
findings.recommendation: No action; confirm orchestrator cursor semantics are intended.

residual_risks: Low. Boundary is metadata-only and maximally fail-closed (all write/permission/authz/audit/security-trimming flags pinned false across policy, module, fixture, contract, and result assertion; every truthy boundary claim rejected; upstream CP00-068 result and RP00.P06.M02.S05 handoff verified; LDIP implementation blocked). Remaining risk is limited to two untested defensive branches and a documentation-vs-enforcement list duplication, none of which can relax the runtime boundary. Diff-level review only: existence/contents of referenced cp00-068 evidence files and the per-subphase construction-inspection.json artifacts (now required by the validator to carry control_plane_permission_audit_access_context_matrix_verified=true) were not verifiable from the diff and should be confirmed present at closeout.
parsed_review.overall_summary: CP00-069 'Permission Audit Access Context Matrix' (Risk A, RP00.P06.M02.S05-RP00.P06.M03.S03, 10 units) is a planning/metadata-only pack that is internally consistent and fully fail-closed. The assembler constructs a frozen descriptive matrix from policy constants, verifies the upstream CP00-068 bootstrap result (pack id, RP00.P06.M02.S05 handoff, prior bindings, no raw policy/internal audit payload, LDIP not allowed), and rejects every truthy boundary claim via a catch-all (forbidden -> 'cannot claim', anything else -> 'cannot accept unknown claim'). All runtime/permission/authz/security-trimming/audit-write/db/storage/product-state/idempotency/lock flags are pinned false in policy, module, fixture, contract, and result assertion; LDIP stays planning-only (ldip_implementation_allowed=false, ldip_implementation_claim fail-closed). Contract closeouts, fixture, policy result_fields, validators, tests, and the contract-validator script are mutually consistent (4 access rows, 1 primary impl row, 5 decision bindings, unit_count 10, next_subphase RP00.P06.M03.S04, M02 completed / M03 open). No P0/P1/P2 issues; only minor non-blocking test-coverage and provenance observations.
